FDA Flu Vaccine Safety Label Changes - Febrile Seizure Warnings

According to Kaitlyn Rivard, a clinical pharmacy specialist in pediatric infectious disease who spoke on behalf of the Society of Infectious Disease Pharmacists, surveillance studies from many past flu seasons have not shown a statistically significant risk of febrile seizure. The published study shows that for every million doses of vaccine given, about 21 additional seizures occurred. By comparison, Rivard notes that about 5% of children hospitalized for influenza will have febrile seizures (a rate of 50,000 for every million) (J Pediatr. 2021, DOI: 10.1016/j.jpeds.2021.06.075).

articles.title: Establishing Priority Pediatric Antimicrobial Stewardship Interventions in the US: Findings from a Delphi Consensus Study
articles.key_finding: Seven priority pediatric antimicrobial stewardship interventions were identified through Delphi consensus, clustered into care settings, prescription patterns, and outcome-tracking strategies.

articles.title: The Era of "Infectious Diseases+" Has Arrived: Multi-disciplinary Integration in Pediatric Infectious Disease Prevention and Control
articles.key_finding: A new "Infectious Diseases+" paradigm integrating artificial intelligence, therapeutics, and social sciences is proposed as essential for managing contemporary pediatric infectious disease prevention and control.

articles.title: Severe Group A Streptococcus Infections in French Children Study
articles.key_finding: Pediatric GAS infections in France show predominance of severe respiratory tract infections (55%) over skin infections (16%), with 3.5% case-fatality rate and M1UK clone representing 50% of emm1 strains.

articles.title: Emerging Scheffersomyces spartinae Fungal Infections in Pakistani Neonates
articles.key_finding: S. spartinae emerged as a novel fungal pathogen in Pakistani neonates and young infants, identified through whole-genome sequencing after misidentification by conventional methods.

articles.title: FDA Flu Vaccine Safety Label Changes - Febrile Seizure Warnings
articles.key_finding: FDA required prominent febrile seizure warnings on five pediatric flu vaccines despite disputed clinical significance, with only 21 additional seizures per million doses compared to 50,000 per million in hospitalized influenza cases.

articles.title: FDA Expands Palynziq (pegvaliase) for Adolescent PKU
articles.key_finding: FDA approved pegvaliase (Palynziq) for adolescents age 12 and older with phenylketonuria, expanding treatment options beyond sapropterin.

articles.title: Pediatric Antimicrobial Stewardship: Current Evidence and Emerging Challenges
articles.key_finding: Core pediatric antimicrobial stewardship interventions including prospective audit and feedback, preauthorization, guideline implementation, and diagnostic stewardship with rapid molecular diagnostics and biomarkers are essential to combat antimicrobial resistance, though implementation barriers and limited pediatric-specific data remain significant challenges.

articles.title: Beyond Traditional Pathogens: Clinical and Microbiologic Insights Into Atypical Pediatric Otitis Media
articles.key_finding: Atypical otopathogens, particularly Turicella otitidis, are increasingly identified in pediatric otitis media and are significantly associated with older age compared to typical pathogens.

articles.title: Antibiotic Exposure and New Diagnosis of Juvenile Idiopathic Arthritis: A Nested Case-Control Study
articles.key_finding: Antibiotic exposure is associated with increased risk of new juvenile idiopathic arthritis diagnosis in children, with dose-response relationships showing stronger associations with repeated exposures and exposures within 0-1 month of diagnosis.

articles.title: FDA Flu Vaccine Safety Label Changes - Febrile Seizure Warnings
articles.key_finding: FDA labeling changes for pediatric influenza vaccines acknowledge febrile seizure risk of approximately 21 additional seizures per million doses, substantially lower than the 50,000 per million rate observed in children hospitalized with influenza.

articles.title: FDA Expands Meningococcal Vaccine for Infants
articles.key_finding: FDA approved expanded indication for quadrivalent meningococcal vaccine (MenQuadfi) for infants aged 6 weeks to 23 months, making it the only MenACWY vaccine approved for use from 6 weeks of age with no upper age limit.

articles.title: FDA Rare Pediatric Disease Priority Review Vouchers Restored
articles.key_finding: The Mikaela Naylon Give Kids a Chance Act was reauthorized and the rare pediatric disease priority review voucher program was revived with funding extended through September 2029 to accelerate development of drugs for rare childhood diseases.

articles.title: IDSA/PIDS 2026 Guidelines for Community-Acquired Pneumonia in Children: Management of Parapneumonic Effusion and Empyema
articles.key_finding: In children with pneumonia-associated empyema, chest tube drainage with intrapleural fibrinolytics using small-bore tubes and tPA monotherapy is recommended as first-line therapy over surgical debridement.

articles.title: Impact of Metagenomic Next-Generation Sequencing on Antibiotic Management in Pediatric Patients
articles.key_finding: mNGS demonstrated positive clinical impact in 39.1% of pediatric patients, particularly immunosuppressed patients (83.3% of those with positive impact), primarily through antimicrobial modifications and new fungal diagnoses.

articles.title: Epidemiology and Characteristics of Bacterial Meningitis in Children with Cerebrospinal Fluid Leakage or Cochlear Implant
articles.journal: Journal of the Pediatric Infectious Diseases Society
articles.key_finding: Bacterial meningitis in children with CSF leakage is predominantly pneumococcal and increasingly caused by serotypes not covered by PCV13, highlighting the need for broader pneumococcal vaccine coverage in this high-risk population.

articles.title: Global Study Estimates Over 250,000 Meningitis Deaths in 2023, With Young Children Bearing Heavy Toll
articles.key_finding: Global meningitis caused 259,000 deaths and 2.5 million infections in 2023, with progress toward WHO targets lagging behind other vaccine-preventable diseases despite significant vaccine-driven improvements since 2000.

articles.title: FDA Expands Meningococcal Vaccine Indication for Infants
articles.key_finding: FDA approval expanded Sanofi's MenQuadfi to infants 6 weeks and older, making it the only MenACWY vaccine approved for this age group and providing coverage against the increasingly prevalent meningococcal serotype Y.

articles.title: Guidelines for Complicated Urinary Tract Infections in Children: A Review by the European Society for Pediatric Infectious Diseases
articles.key_finding: The guideline addresses five distinct categories of complicated UTIs in children under 18 years with evidence-based recommendations to reduce unnecessary variation in care.

articles.title: 2026 IDSA/PIDS Guidelines for Community-Acquired Pneumonia in Infants and Children: Management of Parapneumonic Effusion and Empyema
articles.key_finding: For pneumonia-associated empyema requiring drainage, the panel recommends chest tube drainage combined with intrapleural fibrinolytics rather than immediate surgical debridement, and suggests observation over invasive drainage for small uncomplicated parapneumonic effusions in clinically stable children.

articles.title: Invasive Pneumococcal Disease at Eight Children's Hospitals in the United States, 2018–2023
articles.key_finding: PCV13 serotypes accounted for 32% of IPD in children under 5 years and the 7 new PCV20 serotypes accounted for 25% of IPD in this age group during 2018-2023.

articles.title: Impact of Maternal Immunization Against Respiratory Syncytial Virus on Hospitalizations Due to Lower Respiratory Tract Infections in Infants: A Multicenter Study in Argentina
articles.key_finding: Maternal RSV immunization implemented in Argentina in 2024 was associated with substantial reductions in LRTI and RSV-related hospitalizations among infants under 6 months of age.

articles.title: Risk factors for pediatric invasive pneumococcal disease complicated by purulent meningitis
articles.key_finding: Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio and procalcitonin levels can be used to develop predictive models for identifying children with invasive pneumococcal disease at highest risk for meningitis complications.

articles.title: Tetanus in Four Children — Idaho, Minnesota, Missouri, and Wisconsin, 2024
articles.key_finding: Four cases of tetanus were identified in children across multiple U.S. states in 2024, representing a concerning resurgence of this preventable disease.

articles.title: Clusters of Invasive Haemophilus influenzae Type b Disease Among Adults Using Substances or Experiencing Homelessness or Housing Instability — Alaska, Oregon, and Washington, 2023─2025
articles.key_finding: Clusters of invasive Haemophilus influenzae type b disease were identified among adults with substance use and housing instability in the Pacific Northwest, with potential implications for household transmission risk to pediatric contacts.

articles.title: Pediatric Antimicrobial Stewardship: Current Evidence and Emerging Challenges
articles.key_finding: Multiplex molecular diagnostics have potential to modify therapy in 80% of cases but actual antimicrobial changes occur in only 46%, with escalation more frequent than de-escalation, highlighting implementation gaps in pediatric stewardship.

articles.title: Snowshoe Hare Virus Pediatric Meningoencephalitis Cluster
articles.key_finding: A cluster of three confirmed and probable SSHV meningoencephalitis cases in Whistler, British Columbia represents one of the largest pediatric clusters of SSHV neuroinvasive disease reported in recent years.

articles.title: Coxsackievirus A9 Cardiomyopathy Case
articles.key_finding: Whole-genome sequencing identified a recombinant mosaic coxsackievirus A9 strain causing severe inflammatory cardiomyopathy in a previously healthy child, expanding the recognized spectrum of enterovirus-associated cardiac disease beyond coxsackievirus B serotypes.

articles.title: FDA Expands Meningococcal Vaccine Indication for Infants
articles.key_finding: FDA approved MenQuadfi (meningococcal quadrivalent vaccine) for infants as young as 6 weeks of age, providing the first MenACWY vaccine option for the highest-risk pediatric population.

articles.title: A decade-long real-world cohort (2016–2025): development of an individualized risk-stratification nomogram and evaluation of clinical utility for recurrent respiratory tract infections in children
articles.key_finding: A validated nomogram integrating clinical history, environmental exposure, and nutritional indicators demonstrated good discrimination and clinical utility for predicting 1-year risk of recurrent respiratory tract infections in children.

articles.title: 2026 Surviving Sepsis Campaign Pediatric Guidelines: Implementation Update
articles.key_finding: Updated 2026 SSC pediatric guidelines recommend crystalloids over albumin for initial resuscitation and identify that 30-40% of children surviving ICU-level sepsis treatment may face lasting health issues.

articles.title: Pediatric Antimicrobial Stewardship: Current Evidence and Emerging Challenges
articles.key_finding: Multiplex molecular diagnostics impact on antimicrobial use varies substantially by specimen type and clinical setting, with artificial intelligence emerging as a potential tool to optimize pediatric antimicrobial decision-making pending further validation.

articles.title: Pertussis Outbreak Analysis in South Korea
articles.key_finding: School-based pertussis outbreak investigation found a mean serial interval of 14.7 days with public health interventions reducing the effective reproduction number by 65%.

articles.title: Dengue Transmission in Los Angeles
articles.key_finding: Fourteen locally acquired dengue cases were identified in Los Angeles County during August-November 2024, with cases identified in children as young as 5 years, highlighting emerging dengue transmission in nonendemic U.S. areas.

articles.title: Pertussis Resurgence in Vaccinated Populations: A 2026 Outbreak Investigation
articles.key_finding: Pertussis outbreaks continue to occur in highly vaccinated populations, suggesting waning immunity and need for booster strategies in adolescents and adults.

articles.title: Antimicrobial Stewardship Programs in Pediatric Settings: 2026 Update and Implementation Strategies
articles.key_finding: Multi-faceted stewardship interventions including diagnostic stewardship and prescriber feedback reduce unnecessary antibiotic use by 25-40% in pediatric hospitals.

articles.title: 2026 Updated Good Practice Recommendations for Outpatient Parenteral Antimicrobial Therapy (OPAT) in Adults and Children in the UK
articles.key_finding: Updated BSAC recommendations support formal OPAT service delivery models with structured governance, demonstrating carer-administration as cost-effective and patient-preferred for selected children with defined safety protocols.

articles.title: Group A Streptococcus Disease Outbreak Associated with a Large Congregate Shelter, Chicago, Illinois, USA, October 2023–January 2024
articles.key_finding: Attack rate of 38.8% in a large congregate family shelter with one pediatric death from invasive GAS, highlighting the critical need for retesting in symptomatic children with initial negative RADT results during ongoing GAS outbreaks.

articles.title: Outbreak of Wickerhamomyces anomalus (formerly Candida pelliculosa) Bloodstream Infections, Venezuela, 2022–2023
articles.key_finding: A multi-institutional outbreak of 110 W. anomalus bloodstream infections across 8 Venezuelan hospitals, predominantly affecting neonates in NICUs, demonstrates the emerging threat of underrecognized non-Candida yeasts in resource-limited settings with preserved antifungal susceptibility.

articles.title: Wickerhamomyces anomalus Fungemia during Healthcare-Associated Outbreak, Pereira, Colombia, 2025
articles.key_finding: Single-center outbreak of 10 W. anomalus cases in Pereira, Colombia, affecting predominantly neonates and one young child, with successful echinocandin-based treatment and preserved antifungal susceptibility patterns.

articles.title: Identification of Novel Recombinant Human Adenovirus Genotype B117 from Pediatric Cases, China
articles.key_finding: Identification of a novel recombinant human adenovirus (HAdV-B117) with capsid protein substitutions and similar in vitro replication kinetics to HAdV-B3/B7, emphasizing the need for continuous genomic surveillance to detect emerging recombinants.

articles.title: Nirsevimab Effectiveness and RSV Epidemiology in Infants: 2024 Results from the Australian Sentinel Hospital Network (FluCAN-PAEDS)
articles.key_finding: Nirsevimab demonstrated 82–90% effectiveness against RSV hospitalization in infants, with maternal vaccine effectiveness at 80.8% and nirsevimab at 89.5%, consistent with prior effectiveness estimates and supporting continued implementation.

articles.title: Characteristics of Patients Hospitalized with Measles During an Outbreak — West Texas, January–March 2025
articles.journal: MMWR Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report
articles.key_finding: Of 325 measles cases in West Texas, 60 (18.5%) were hospitalized—predominantly unvaccinated children with pneumonia occurring in 72% of hospitalized patients—demonstrating severe disease even in previously healthy infants and children.

articles.title: Measles Outbreak in a Child Care Facility — Lubbock, Texas, March–April 2025
articles.journal: MMWR Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report
articles.key_finding: Eight confirmed measles cases in a Texas childcare facility prompted emergency modification of MMR timing (first dose 6–11 months) and illustrated the critical vulnerability of infants under 12 months who rely on herd immunity in low-vaccination communities.

articles.title: Modeled Scenario Projections and Risk Assessment for the Ebola Disease Outbreak Caused by Bundibugyo Virus, 2026
articles.journal: MMWR Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report
articles.key_finding: As of June 2, 2026, 378 confirmed BVD cases and 63 deaths were reported; CDC modeling showed effective case isolation (70% entry rate) would prevent outbreak exceeding 10,000 cases in 3 months; U.S. pediatric risk assessed as low with emphasis on supportive care given absence of approved vaccine or treatment.

articles.title: BCG Vaccination Associated with Reduced Risk of PFAPA Syndrome — Nationwide Matched Case-Control Study
articles.key_finding: Nationwide Israeli matched case-control study (N=459,993) found BCG vaccination associated with reduced PFAPA risk, suggesting potential non-specific immune benefit and earlier diagnosis recognition that could reduce unnecessary antibiotic courses.
